name: take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351
idea:
Take the video editor timeline state management from 'cleaned up' to 'excellently engineered'. Also audit and fix inefficiencies, race conditions, and rough edges across timeline and preview rendering.

## What was just done (previous refactor)
- useTimelineSave decomposed into useTimelineCommit (314 lines), useTimelinePersistence (370 lines), usePollSync (288 lines)
- Unified applyEdit with discriminated union replaces dual commit paths
- Default-reject poll model via isTimelinePollIdle()
- resolveGroupOverlaps now returns metaPatches
- useTimelineData eliminated, types in useTimelineData.types.ts
- AudioTrack composite key for Remotion remount

## Remaining structural problems

### 1. useTimelineState is 523 lines and does too much
It composes 13+ hooks, builds a 60+ property context, and contains inline dataHook assembly (lines 86-136) that's just the old useTimelineData pass-through rewritten inline. It's the new mega-hook.

### 2. TimelineEditorContextValue is a god interface (~65 properties)
Components that need scale+dataRef get the entire kitchen. The existing Editor/Chrome/Playback split is good but Editor needs further decomposition.

### 3. Four mutable refs bridge hooks that can't share scope
onSaveSuccessRef, onBeforeCommitRef, pruneSelectionRef, scheduleSaveRef — fragile ref-wiring because React hooks don't share mutable state well. A proper state container (zustand store or useReducer) would eliminate these.

### 4. useDerivedTimeline signature cache still uses mutable refs
The ref-based configSignatureRef/stableResolvedConfigRef pattern adds indirection. With default-reject polls now in place, this may be simplifiable.

## Inefficiencies and race conditions to audit

### 5. Preview/Remotion rendering efficiency
- TimelineRenderer is memo-wrapped but rebuilds clipsByTrack on every config change
- RemotionPreview creates inputProps via useMemo but the Player may still re-render unnecessarily
- The metadata computation (durationInFrames) runs on every config change even when only clip content changed, not duration

### 6. Timeline canvas rendering
- TimelineCanvas is 600+ lines — check for unnecessary re-renders during drag
- ClipAction re-renders during drag even when its own props haven't changed
- The drag coordinator uses requestAnimationFrame batching but the indicator position update may cause layout thrashing

### 7. Potential remaining race conditions
- useClipResize: does it also need pendingOpsRef increment during resize? (Same pattern as drag)
- useExternalDrop: external drops during polling — is this protected?
- useTimelineHistory: undo/redo during save — could this create version conflicts?
- useAssetManagement: concurrent asset operations — are these properly serialized?

## Goals
1. Introduce a proper state container (zustand or useReducer) to replace the ref-wiring pattern
2. Split TimelineEditorContextValue into focused sub-contexts within the existing Editor/Chrome/Playback structure  
3. Slim useTimelineState by moving concerns into the state container
4. Audit and fix rendering inefficiencies in preview and timeline canvas
5. Audit and fix any remaining race conditions (resize, external drop, undo during save)
6. Simplify useDerivedTimeline if safe with default-reject polls
7. Keep all 22 test files / 71 tests passing

## Key files (all in reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/)
- hooks/useTimelineState.ts (523 lines) — the new mega-hook
- hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts — god interface
- hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts (314 lines) — commit/edit state
- hooks/useTimelinePersistence.ts (370 lines) — save queue
- hooks/usePollSync.ts (288 lines) — poll sync
- hooks/useDerivedTimeline.ts — signature cache
- hooks/useClipDrag.ts — drag with pendingOpsRef
- hooks/useClipResize.ts — resize (no pendingOpsRef?)
- hooks/useExternalDrop.ts — external drops
- compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx — Remotion rendering
- compositions/AudioTrack.tsx — audio rendering
- components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx — preview player
- components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx — timeline UI
- components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx — clip rendering
- contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx — provider mounting

Test command: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/
